title: Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)

Const INSURANCE_REQUEST_MSG As String = "Contractor is not insured. Create INSURANCE Request?"
Const DBS_REQUEST_MSG As String = "Contractor has not been DBS’d. Create DBS Request?"
Const INSURANCE_DBS_REQUEST_MSG As String = "Contractor is not insured and has not been DBS’d. Create INSURANCE/DBS Request?"
Const ADMIN_REQUEST_EXISTS__MSG As String = "Contractor has already been sent an Admin Request. Create ADMIN/DBS Request?"

Dim answer As VbMsgBoxResult
Dim insured As Boolean
Dim cleared As Boolean
Dim contractor As String
Dim exists As String

'CHECK AND MAKE SURE ONLY ONE CELL IS SELECTED
If Target.CountLarge > 1 Then Exit Sub
'CHECK IF CHANGE HAPPENED IN A2
If Not Intersect(Target, Range("A2")) Is Nothing Then
Application.EnableEvents = False
ActiveSheet.Calculate 'recalculate sheet
Application.EnableEvents = True

'IF CHANGE HAPPENED IN A2 THEN SHOW RELEVANT MESSAGE DEPENDING ON THE VALUES IN G3 AND H3
CHECKS:
insured = (Range("G3").Value <> "")
cleared = (Range("H3").Value <> "")
contractor = Range("B3").Value
If Not insured And Not cleared Then
answer = MsgBox(Format(INSURANCE_DBS_REQUEST_MSG, contractor), vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Send details")
If answer = vbYes Then
Call CopyToWorkPermit
Exit Sub
End If
ElseIf Not insured Then 'only G3 is empty
answer = MsgBox(Format(INSURANCE_REQUEST_MSG, contractor), vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Send details")
If answer = vbYes Then
Call CopyToWorkPermit
Exit Sub
End If
ElseIf Not cleared Then 'only H3 is empty
answer = MsgBox(Format(DBS_REQUEST_MSG, contractor), vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Send details")
If answer = vbYes Then
Call CopyToWorkPermit
Exit Sub
End If
CHECKS COMPLETE:
End If

COMPLETE:
'both G3 and H3 have values
'IF ANSWER IS YES MOVE TO THE WORK PERMIT SHEET AND EXIT THE SUB
'IF ANSWER IS NO REMAIN IN CURRENT SHEET
'IF CHANGE HAPPENED IN F2 THEN CHECK IF ALL 6 CELLS IN A2:F2 HAVE VALUES
If Not Intersect(Target, Range("F2")) Is Nothing Then
If Application.CountA(Range("A2:F2")) = 6 Then
'WHEN ALL CELLS HAVE VALUES CALL THE REQUEST SUB
Call Request
ElseIf Application.CountA(Range("A2:F2")) <> 6 Then
'SHOW PROMPT "ALL FIELDS NEED TO BE FILLED IN
MsgBox ("All fields need to be filled in")
SelectFirstEmptyCell
End If
End If
End If
End Sub

Private Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim LastCol As Long
Set ws = ActiveSheet
Application.EnableEvents = False
LastCol = ws.Cells(2, Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
ws.Cells(2, LastCol + 1).Select
Application.EnableEvents = True
End Sub

From the routines provided above, please adjust and optimise the code to comply with the following instructions.

If change happens in A2 of Sheet Job Request, recalculate the page and check all values of Column B in the Sheeet named in I2.
If the value 'Admin request' is not present in the values of Column B in the Sheeet named in I2 then continue the routine between the label CHECKS: and CHECKS COMPLETE:
If the value 'Admin request' is present, then
Pop up a messages that asks "Admin Request has already been sent. Do you want to re-send".
If I enter yes, then continue with the rest of the routine between the label CHECKS: and CHECKS COMPLETE:
If I enetr no, do nothing until change happens in F2.
If there is change in F2 and A2:F2 <> 6 then call routine SelectFirstEmptyCell
and wait until A2:F2 <> 6.
If there is change in F2 and A2:F2 = 6 then call routine Request

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)

Const INSURANCE_REQUEST_MSG As String = "Contractor is not insured. Create INSURANCE Request?"
Const DBS_REQUEST_MSG As String = "Contractor has not been DBS’d. Create DBS Request?"
Const INSURANCE_DBS_REQUEST_MSG As String = "Contractor is not insured and has not been DBS’d. Create INSURANCE/DBS Request?"
Const ADMIN_REQUEST_EXISTS_MSG As String = "Admin Request has already been sent. Do you want to re-send?"
Const ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG As String = "Admin Request"

Dim answer As VbMsgBoxResult
Dim insured As Boolean
Dim cleared As Boolean
Dim contractor As String
Dim exists As String
Dim ws As Worksheet

'Set the worksheet that contains the Admin Requests
Set ws = Sheets(Range("I2").Value)

'CHECK AND MAKE SURE ONLY ONE CELL IS SELECTED
If Target.CountLarge > 1 Then Exit Sub

'CHECK IF CHANGE HAPPENED IN A2
If Not Intersect(Target, Range("A2")) Is Nothing Then
    Application.EnableEvents = False
    ActiveSheet.Calculate 'recalculate sheet
    Application.EnableEvents = True

    'CHECK IF ADMIN REQUEST EXISTS
    exists = Application.WorksheetFunction.Match(ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG, ws.Range("B:B"), 0)
    
    'IF ADMIN REQUEST EXISTS SHOW PROMPT
    If Not IsError(exists) Then
        answer = MsgBox(ADMIN_REQUEST_EXISTS_MSG, vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Resend details")
        If answer = vbNo Then
            Exit Sub
        End If
    End If

    'IF CHANGE HAPPENED IN A2 THEN SHOW RELEVANT MESSAGE DEPENDING ON THE VALUES IN G3 AND H3
    CHECKS:
    insured = (Range("G3").Value <> "")
    cleared = (Range("H3").Value <> "")
    contractor = Range("B3").Value
    
    If Not insured And Not cleared Then
        answer = MsgBox(Format(INSURANCE_DBS_REQUEST_MSG, contractor), vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Send details")
        If answer = vbYes Then
            Call CopyToWorkPermit
            Exit Sub
        End If
    ElseIf Not insured Then 'only G3 is empty
        answer = MsgBox(Format(INSURANCE_REQUEST_MSG, contractor), vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Send details")
        If answer = vbYes Then
            Call CopyToWorkPermit
            Exit Sub
        End If
    ElseIf Not cleared Then 'only H3 is empty
        answer = MsgBox(Format(DBS_REQUEST_MSG, contractor), vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Send details")
        If answer = vbYes Then
            Call CopyToWorkPermit
            Exit Sub
        End If
    END CHECKS:

END IF

COMPLETE:
'both G3 and H3 have values
'IF ANSWER IS YES MOVE TO THE WORK PERMIT SHEET AND EXIT THE SUB
'IF ANSWER IS NO REMAIN IN CURRENT SHEET
'IF CHANGE HAPPENED IN F2 THEN CHECK IF ALL 6 CELLS IN A2:F2 HAVE VALUES
If Not Intersect(Target, Range("F2")) Is Nothing Then
    If Application.CountA(Range("A2:F2")) = 6 Then
        'WHEN ALL CELLS HAVE VALUES CALL THE REQUEST SUB
        Call Request
    ElseIf Application.CountA(Range("A2:F2")) <> 6 Then
        'SHOW PROMPT "ALL FIELDS NEED TO BE FILLED IN
        MsgBox ("All fields need to be filled in")
        SelectFirstEmptyCell
    End If
End If
End Sub

Private Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim LastCol As Long
Set ws = ActiveSheet
Application.EnableEvents = False
LastCol = ws.Cells(2, Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
ws.Cells(2, LastCol + 1).Select
Application.EnableEvents = True
End Sub

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Const ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG As String = "Admin Request"
Dim exists As String
If Not Intersect(Target, Range("A2")) Is Nothing Then
Application.EnableEvents = False
ActiveSheet.Calculate 'recalculate sheet
Application.EnableEvents = True
Application.Wait (Now + TimeValue("0:00:01"))
'Set the worksheet that contains the Admin Requests
Dim ws As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)

'CHECK IF ADMIN REQUEST EXISTS
exists = Application.WorksheetFunction.Match(ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG, ws.Range("B:B"), 0)

'IF ADMIN REQUEST EXISTS SHOW PROMPT
If Not IsError(exists) Then
answer = MsgBox(ADMIN_REQUEST_EXISTS_MSG, vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Resend details")
If answer = vbNo Then
Exit Sub

The vba code line,
exists = Application.WorksheetFunction.Match(ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG, ws.Range("B:B"), 0)
is giving me this error.
Run-time error '1004':
Unable to get the Match property of the WorksheetFunction class

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Try updating the line to the following code to handle the error:
```
On Error Resume Next
exists = Application.WorksheetFunction.Match(ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG, ws.Range("B:B"), 0)
On Error GoTo 0
If IsError(exists) Then exists = CVErr(xlErrNA)
```
This should handle the error if the Match function does not find a match and set the exists variable to a CV Error so that it can be checked for later. This will prevent the code from crashing and allow you to handle the error appropriately.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: what does this line of code do: exists = Application.WorksheetFunction.Match(ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG, ws.Range("B:B"), 0)

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
This line of code uses the Match function from the WorksheetFunction object in VBA to determine if the value stored in the ADMIN_REQUEST_PRESENT_MSG constant is present in column B of the worksheet specified in the I2 cell. 

The Match function returns the position of the first occurrence of a value within a range, or an error value if the value is not found. In this case, the function is set with the third argument as 0, which means it performs an exact match. 

The result is stored in the exists variable. If the value exists in column B, exists will be an integer representing the row number (i.e., the position of the first occurrence of the value), and if it doesn't exist, it will return an error value.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Can i have a line of code to determine if the value "Admin Request" is present in column B of the worksheet specified in the I2 cell.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Yes, you can use the `Range.Find` method to determine if "Admin Request" is present in column B of the specified worksheet. Here's an example code snippet that uses `Range.Find` to check if "Admin Request" is present in Column B of the worksheet specified in cell I2:

```
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim adminRequestCell As Range

Set ws = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
Set adminRequestCell = ws.Range("B:B").Find("Admin Request", , xlValues, xlWhole, xlByColumns)

If Not adminRequestCell Is Nothing Then
    ' "Admin Request" is found in Column B
    ' Do something...
Else
    ' "Admin Request" is not found in Column B
    ' Do something else...
End If
```

The `Range.Find` method searches for the value "Admin Request" in the entire B column and returns a `Range` object that represents the first cell found with that value. If the value is not found, `adminRequestCell` is set to `Nothing`. The `If` statement checks if `adminRequestCell` is not `Nothing`, indicating that the value "Admin Request" was found in column B.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
In Worksheets(Range("I2").Value), and Range("B:B"), the Value "Admin Request" does not exist.
The code below does not detect that the Value "Admin Request" does not exist using the lines of code below.
Can you detect the error in the code?

Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim adminRequestCell As Range
Set ws = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
Set adminRequestCell = ws.Range("B:B").Find("Admin Request", , xlValues, xlWhole, xlByColumns)
If Not adminRequestCell Is Nothing Then
' "Admin Request" is found in Column B in the Contractors Sheet
answer = MsgBox(ADMIN_REQUEST_EXISTS_MSG, vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Resend details")
If answer = vbNo Then
GoTo COMPLETE
Else
' "Admin Request" is not found in Column B in the Contractors Sheet
GoTo CHECKS
End If

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The problem with the code is that, even when `adminRequestCell` is `Nothing`, it jumps to the `CHECKS` label directly without showing the message box. This means that it would falsely assume that "Admin Request" does not exist in the column B of the worksheet specified in cell I2 and therefore, would always execute the `CHECKS` label. 

To fix this, you can use an additional flag variable to indicate if "Admin Request" was found, and adjust the code as follows:

```
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim adminRequestCell As Range
Dim isAdminRequestPresent as Boolean

Set ws = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
Set adminRequestCell = ws.Range("B:B").Find("Admin Request", , xlValues, xlWhole, xlByColumns)
If Not adminRequestCell Is Nothing Then
    ' "Admin Request" is found in Column B in the Contractors Sheet
    isAdminRequestPresent = True
    answer = MsgBox(ADMIN_REQUEST_EXISTS_MSG, vbYesNo + vbQuestion, "Resend details")
    If answer = vbNo Then
        GoTo COMPLETE
    End If
End If

If Not isAdminRequestPresent Then
    GoTo CHECKS
End If

' rest of the code goes here
```

In this updated code, before showing the message box, `isAdminRequestPresent` flag is set to true if the value is found in the column B. Next, if the user responds "no" to the message box, the code jumps to the `COMPLETE` label as before.

After this section of the code, we check if the `isAdminRequestPresent` flag is set. If it is not set, the code jumps to the `CHECKS` label as before. However, if the flag is set, the code will continue executing after this section, and the routine between the CHECKS and CHECKS COMPLETE labels will not be executed, as expected.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Can you please adjust this code so that it selects the first empty cell with B2 and E2.   Private Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim LastCol As Long
Set ws = ActiveSheet
Application.EnableEvents = False
LastCol = ws.Cells(2, Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
ws.Cells(2, LastCol + 1).Select
Application.EnableEvents = True
End Sub

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Sure, you can modify the `SelectFirstEmptyCell` subroutine to find the first empty cell in columns B and E instead of the last used cell:

```
Private Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim nextRow As Long
    
    Set ws = ActiveSheet
    Application.EnableEvents = False
    nextRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1 ' Find next empty cell in column B
    If ws.Cells(nextRow, "B").Value <> "" Then ' Check if the cell in column B is empty
        nextRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "E").End(xlUp).Row + 1 ' If column B is not empty, find next empty cell in column E
    End If
    ws.Cells(nextRow, "B").Select ' Select the first empty cell in column B or E, whichever is found first
    Application.EnableEvents = True
End Sub
```

In this modified code, we are first finding the last used cell in column B using `Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp)`, and adding 1 to get the row number of the next empty cell. We then check if the cell in column B is empty (`ws.Cells(nextRow, "B").Value <> ""`). If it is not empty, we repeat the process to find the next empty cell in column E. Finally, we select the first empty cell that was found, either in column B or E.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I only want to check starting from B2, for the first empty cell within the range B2, C2, D2, E2, then selecting the first empty cell found and then exiting the sub

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
You can modify the `SelectFirstEmptyCell` subroutine to start the search from cell B2 and only check the cells B2:E2:

```
Private Sub SelectFirstEmptyCell()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim nextCell As Range
    
    Set ws = ActiveSheet
    Application.EnableEvents = False
    Set nextCell = ws.Range("B2:E2").Find("", , xlValues, xlWhole) ' Find the first empty cell (exact matching) within the range B2:E2
    If Not nextCell Is Nothing Then ' If an empty cell is found, select it
        nextCell.Select
    End If
    Application.EnableEvents = True
End Sub
```
In this modified code, we use the `Range.Find` method to search for the first empty cell (exact matching) within the range B2:E2. We set the `nextCell` variable to the first empty cell that is found, or `Nothing` if no empty cells are found. If an empty cell is found, we select it. Finally, we enable events and exit the subroutine.
